task_completion.description: Navigate to the DOT Airline Customer Service Dashboard
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox browser open and displaying the official US Department of Transportation (transportation.gov) Airline Customer Service Dashboard.

task_completion.description: Download the 'Fly Rights' or 'Consumer Guide to Air Travel' PDF
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Firefox download manager or a file manager window showing a PDF file related to 'Fly Rights' or 'Consumer Guide to Air Travel' successfully downloaded to the ~/Downloads/ directory.

task_completion.description: Create a bookmark folder named 'Travel Policy Resources'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Firefox bookmark manager, bookmark toolbar, or sidebar showing a newly created folder explicitly named 'Travel Policy Resources'.

task_completion.description: Bookmark the DOT Dashboard and Tarmac Delay pages
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Travel Policy Resources' bookmark folder is shown containing at least two bookmarks: one for the DOT Dashboard and one for the 'Tarmac Delay' consumer page.

task_completion.description: Create the airline_rights_audit.json file in the Documents folder
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file manager or text editor showing a file named 'airline_rights_audit.json' located in the ~/Documents/ directory.

task_completion.description: Populate the JSON file with the required airlines and boolean keys
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor showing the contents of 'airline_rights_audit.json' formatted as valid JSON. It must include the airlines 'American', 'Delta', 'Southwest', and 'Spirit', and each must have the boolean keys 'meal_voucher', 'hotel_accommodation', and 'rebook_partner'.

integrity.description: Agent used the Firefox GUI to access the DOT website and download the PDF
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Firefox browser interface being used to navigate to transportation.gov and initiate the PDF download, rather than using terminal commands like curl or wget.

integrity.description: Agent used the Firefox GUI to create bookmarks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Firefox bookmark manager or bookmark star icon being used, rather than terminal commands directly modifying the places.sqlite database.

integrity.description: Agent used a text editor to create and populate the JSON file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a GUI text editor (like gedit, VS Code, or similar) being used to write the JSON file, rather than a single terminal echo command or script.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output JSON file must be located exactly at '/home/ga/Documents/airline_rights_audit.json'. It must contain exactly these airlines: 'American', 'Delta', 'Southwest', 'Spirit'. For each airline, it must contain exactly these keys with boolean values: 'meal_voucher', 'hotel_accommodation', 'rebook_partner'. The bookmark folder must be named 'Travel Policy Resources' and the bookmarked URLs must belong to the 'transportation.gov' domain.
